title: Workhorse Group Flags Going Concern Risk Despite Motiv Merger
text: Workhorse Group flags going concern risk despite Motiv reverse merger and new credit lines. The company warns that failure to secure new financing could force a bankruptcy filing, with heavy 2025 losses and a full draw on a $10M working capital line. The company is actively working to evaluate financing alternatives; however, there is no certainty that a potential equity or equity-linked financing transaction will be consummated. Workhorse's odds of distress is above 80%, with very high probability of going through financial crunch in the upcoming years. The company has an Altman Z-Score of -14.22, indicating it is in Distress Zones with bankruptcy possibility within the next two years. Workhorse Motor Works Inc. entered into a Purchase and Sale Agreement for the sale of its Union City, Indiana manufacturing facility for $20.0 million. As of December 31, 2025, the Company had no outstanding borrowings and remaining availability of $40.0 million under the Customer Order Credit Agreement.
